description_long: This object consists of three large, cylindrical metal barrels, two of which are upright and one is heavily dented and lying on its side. The barrels are constructed from rusted steel, showing significant weathering and corrosion, with a rough, pitted surface texture. Each barrel features three prominent horizontal reinforcement bands spaced evenly along the height, which add structural rigidity. The upright barrels have closed tops and bottoms, while the barrel on its side appears to have a partially open or deformed end, suggesting damage or heavy use. The coloration is predominantly a mottled brownish-rust, with areas of darker and lighter oxidation. There are no visible handles, lids, or external markings, and the barrels are empty, as indicated by the open end of the damaged one.
description: A group of three rusted steel barrels, two upright and one dented and lying on its side, with prominent reinforcement bands and a heavily weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open, deformed end of the damaged barrel in front of two upright barrels.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the dented barrel and the cylindrical shape of the two upright barrels.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the tops of the two upright barrels, with the damaged barrel mostly hidden.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side profile of the dented barrel and the upright barrels' cylindrical forms.
